title: lasianthaea fruticosa var. michoacana
intro: Lasianthaea fruticosa var. michoacana, also known as the Michoacan lasianthaea, is a plant species native to Mexico, classified within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Lasianthaea fruticosa var. michoacana include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Lasianthaea fruticosa var. michoacana is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves, roots, or flowers in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
